zh.Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England: 英國瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林
zh.lifeSummary: 當Ralph le Boteler於1272年出生在英國瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林時，他的父親Ralph le Boteler25歲，母親Maud Marmion10歲。他於1301年1月3日之前與Hawise Gobion結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1342年5月26日在英國史洛普爾諾波利享年70歲。

zh-hans.Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England: 英国瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ralph le Boteler于1272年出生在英国瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林时，他的父亲Ralph le Boteler25岁，母亲Maud Marmion10岁。他于1301年1月3日之前与Hawise Gobion结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1342年5月26日在英国史洛普爾諾波利享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Ralph le Boteler was born in 1272, in Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England, his father, Ralph le Boteler, was 25 and his mother, Maud Marmion, was 10. He married Hawise Gobion before 3 January 1301. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 26 May 1342, in Norbury, Shropshire, England, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ralph le Boteler est né en 1272, à Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Ralph le Boteler, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Maud Marmion, avait 10 ans. Il a épousé Hawise Gobion avant 3 janvier 1301. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 26 mai 1342, à Norbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ralph le Boteler 1272 in Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph le Boteler 25 und seine Mutter Maud Marmion 10. Er hat vor 3. Januar 1301 Hawise Gobion geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 26. Mai 1342 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Norbury, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph le Boteler è nato il nel 1272 nel Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England, il suo padre, Ralph le Boteler, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Maud Marmion, aveva 10 anni. Lui sposato Hawise Gobion prima del 3 gennaio 1301. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 26 maggio 1342, nel Norbury, Shropshire, England, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Ralph le Botelerが1272年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアオバースリー グレンで生まれた時，父Ralph le Botelerは25歳，母Maud Marmionは10歳であった。1301年1月3日以前にHawise Gobionと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1342年5月26日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアノーブリーで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ralph le Boteler(이)가 1272년 영국 워윅실르 버슬리 그린에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph le Boteler(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Marmion(은)는 10세였다. 그는 1301년 1월 3일 이전 Hawise Gobion(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1342년 5월 26일 영국 슈럽실르 노르버이에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph le Boteler nasceu em 1272, em Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph le Boteler, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Maud Marmion, tinha 10 anos. Ele casou-se com Hawise Gobion antes 3 de janeiro de 1301. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 26 de maio de 1342, em Norbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ralph le Boteler родился в 1272 г. в местности овирсли грин, варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Ralph le Boteler было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Marmion, 10. Он женился на женщине по имени Hawise Gobion перед 3 январь 1301 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер 26 мая 1342 г. в местности норбури, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ralph le Boteler nació en 1272, en Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Ralph le Boteler, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Maud Marmion, tenía 10 años. Se casó con Hawise Gobion antes de 3 de enero de 1301. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 26 de mayo de 1342, en Norbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 70 años.
